events.raw_text:

Thu, May 29, 2025 · 5:30 PM to 8:00 PM CDT

dbt Labs

522 Congress Avenue, Suite 700, Austin TX 78701 · Austin, TX

dbt Global Circuit Series: Austin
D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Austin dbt Meetup
4.8
16 reviews
Details

In this meetup, we’ll dive into the latest tools and ideas we’ve been shipping — what they unlock, how they fit together, and why we built them the way we did.

This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers — folks who want to roll up their sleeves (figuratively), learn from each other, and imagine what’s next for the analytics engineering stack.

Whether you’re curious about what’s new, want to see how others are approaching similar problems, or just want to ask hard questions — you’re in the right place.

🤝Organizer: dbt Labs
🏠Venue Host: dbt Labs' Austin Office: 522 Congress Avenue, Suite 700, Austin TX 78701 — Scarbrough Building

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

5:30 - 6:00pm | Check-in, nametags and mingling
6:00 - 6:25pm | For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs” by Zach Ruben (SA, dbt Labs)
6:25 - 6:40 | Q & A
6:40 - 8:00pm | Networking, bites and drinks

Our venue has capacity limits. Please RSVP if you intend to come and change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going" or message Amada Echeverria in Meetup if you have an issue.

EVENT DETAILS:

🚗 Parking info: Littlefield Garage is nearby. Address: 508 Brazos Street Austin Tx, 78701.
The doors open at 5:30pm. If you arrive before, we may not be ready to welcome you yet!

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/ and join the #local-texas channel.

events.page_title: Austin dbt Meetup: The 2025 State of the AE Report, Thu, Apr 17, 2025, 5:30 PM | Meetup
events.raw_text:
Skip to content
Homepage
Start a new group
30% off

Thu, Apr 17, 2025 · 5:30 PM to 8:30 PM CDT

dbt Labs

522 Congress Avenue, Suite 700, Austin TX 78701 · Austin, TX

Austin dbt Meetup: The 2025 State of the AE Report
D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Austin dbt Meetup
4.8
16 reviews
Details

The 2025 State of Analytics Engineering, crafted by dbt Labs, examines how data teams are navigating this new AI era. This report isn’t just a snapshot of where we are today. It’s a blueprint for where analytics engineering is headed next. Let’s dive in.

🤝Organizer: dbt Labs Community Team
🏠Venue Host: dbt Labs' Austin Office: 522 Congress Avenue, Suite 700, Austin TX 78701 — Scarbrough Building

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

5:30 - 6:00pm | Check-in, nametags and mingling
6:00 - 6:50pm | Discussion of the State of the Analytics Engineering Report results with Amada Echeverria (Community @ dbt Labs), Sunny Pachunuri (Director of Data @ Outpost), and Erica Louie (Senior Analytics Manager: Engineering, Product, Design @ dbt Labs), and Nikita Pestrov (Senior Analytics Engineer @ Zello)
6:50 - 7:05 | Q & A
7:05 - 8:30pm | Networking, bites and drinks

Our venue has capacity limits. Please RSVP if you intend to come and change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going" or message Amada Echeverria in Meetup if you have an issue.

PANELISTS
Nikita Pestrov is a Senior Analytics Engineer at Zello, a leading push-to-talk communications platform for frontline teams. Data Leader with over 8 years of experience specializing in developing scalable and impactful data products for clients like Mastercard, the World Bank, and PWC. Skilled in building distributed cloud-native data pipelines, with deep foundation in AI/ML and SaaS product development.

Erica "ric" Louie joined dbt Labs as the second employee in 2017 and became the Head of Data for three years where she built the foundational data team. Currently, she’s in her dream role: managing the data org that supports dbt Labs’s Engineering, Product, and Design vertical (i.e. doing data for the product we all know and love).

Sunny Pachunuri is the Director of Data at Outpost, where he’s building the foundation for data to power every decision—and help predict the future of logistics. With a track record across Fortune 100 companies and early-stage startups, he led the data function at Endpoint from the ground up and now focuses on designing platforms that make insights accessible, actionable, and genuinely transformative. Grounded in believing that great work comes from doing what you love, Sunny brings a high-expectation, high-ownership mindset to everything he builds—driven by a deep curiosity and a passion for solving the problems that matter most. His philosophy is simple: if something’s a problem, it matters—so don’t complain, just solve it.

EVENT DETAILS:

🚗 Parking info: Littlefield Garage is nearby. Address: 508 Brazos Street Austin Tx, 78701.
The doors open at 5:30pm. If you arrive before, we may not be ready to welcome you yet!

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/ and join the #local-texas channel.

Wed, Jan 22, 2025 · 5:30 PM to 8:00 PM CST

dbt Labs

522 Congress Avenue, Suite 700, Austin TX 78701 · Austin, TX

Austin dbt Meetup

Hosted by Tyler R.

Austin dbt Meetup
4.8
16 reviews
Details

What are dbt meetups? dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝Organizer: dbt Labs Community Team
🏠Venue Host: dbt Labs - Austin Office: 522 Congress Avenue, Suite 700, Austin TX 78701 — Scarbrough Building
🍕Catering: light refreshments will be provided

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation:** https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

5:30 - 6:00 | Check in/Registration (30 min)
6:00 - 6:05 | Welcome Remarks by dbt Labs (5 min)
6:05 - 6:35 | Presentation by Nicole Smith (dbt Labs) on Unlocking the Power of Metadata: How dbt Cloud Helps Teams Work Smarter (30 min)
6:35 - 6:45 | Presentation Q&A (10 min)
6:45 - 6:50 | Closing Remarks by dbt Labs + call for speakers, host, and organizers for next time... let's keep this going! (5 min)
6:50 - 8:00 | Networking & Reception

🗣️Presentation Topic: Unlocking the Power of Metadata: How dbt Cloud Helps Teams Work Smarter
Description: Nicole will highlight the existing and new features of dbt that can help you work smarter, such as column level lineage, model query history, advanced CI, Copilot and the visual editor.

EVENT DETAILS:
Food and refreshments will be provided.
🚗 Parking Garage Information: Littlefield Garage is nearby. Address: 508 Brazos Street Austin Tx, 78701.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-texas channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

***

dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more
Speaker
Nicole Smith

Nicole is a Solutions Architect at dbt Labs. She is a dedicated professional with a strong background in data engineering and data visualization, driven by a deep passion for helping others derive value from their data and create a compelling story with it.

events.raw_text:

Thu, Nov 21, 2024 · 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Online event

This event has passed

Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Austin dbt Meetup
4.8
16 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Thu, Oct 3, 2024 · 5:30 PM to 8:30 PM CDT

dbt Labs office - Austin

522 Congress Ave. #700 Austin, TX 78701 · Austin, TX

'dbt Labs on dbt' Meetup: Austin
D

Hosted by dbt L. and Jakki J.

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Austin dbt Meetup
4.8
16 reviews
Details

🤝Organizer: dbt Labs
🏠Venue Host: dbt Labs Office @ 522 Congress Avenue, Suite 700, Austin TX 78701 — Scarbrough Building
🍕Catering: TBD

🗣️Description: In the dbt Labs on dbt series, you’ll get a behind-the-scenes look at how dbt Labs uses our own product. You’ll learn how dbt Labs thinks about the role of data, how data developers collaborate with business leaders, and the technical decisions we’ve made in our own dbt project.

In this session, Brandon Thomson, GTM Analytics Manager at dbt Labs, will share how he’s partnered with both marketing leaders and the data team to deliver clean, simple reporting that help everyone from campaign managers to the executive team make better decisions about marketing spend and optimization. Jakki Jakaj, Sr. Manager of Campaigns at dbt Labs, will share how her team uses this data every day to optimize marketing campaigns.

🎤Speaker bios:
Jakki Jakaj is a Marketing Guru at dbt Labs, based in Austin, TX. A true food enthusiast, Jakki combines her passion for marketing with her love for exploring culinary delights. In her role for over three years, she spearheads impactful Go-to-Market strategies that drive conversions and ensures every user enjoys a remarkable experience with dbt. When not immersed in work, Jakki treasures quality time with friends and family!

Brandon Thomson is a GTM Analytics Manager at dbt Labs. Brandon has been in the analytics space since undergrad, where he received his degree in Business Competitive Intelligence. From there, he moved on to the world of PR & marketing analytics helping some of the world’s largest brands measure the impact of their efforts. Now, he is an Analytics Manager for the Go-to-Market team at dbt Labs. When he’s not thinking about campaign optimization and attribution modeling, Brandon spends his time recreating in the mountains near where he lives in Salt Lake City.

📝 Agenda

5:30 - 6:00 | Check in/Registration (30 min)
6:00 - 6:05 | Welcome Remarks by Meetup Host, Tyler Rouze (dbt Labs)
6:05 - 6:35| Presentation with Jakki Jakkaj (dbt Labs) and Brandon Thomson (dbt Labs) (30 min)
6:35 - 6:45 | Presentation Q&A (10 min)
6:45 - 6:50 | Closing Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)
6:50 - 8:30 | Networking

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-texas channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Tue, Jan 23, 2024 · 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM CST

Capital Factory

701 Brazos St · Austin, TX

How to find us
Austin dbt Meetup

Hosted by Jakki J. and Daniel L.

Austin dbt Meetup
4.8
16 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! dbt allows teams to ship trusted data products, faster. dbt Meetups are a way for people to connect over their experiences with dbt, as well as discuss topics related to the data industry, team dynamics, best practices, and much more.

🏠Venue: Capital Factory - Room: Voltron
🍕Catering: TBA! Expect light bites and beverages
🤝Organizer: dbt Labs' Jakki Jakkaj and Danny Lambert

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation.

📝Agenda

6:00 - 6:30 | Check in/Registration/Refreshments (30 min)
6:30 - 6:35 | Welcome Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)
6:35 - 6:55 | Presentation (~20 min)
6:55 - 7:05 | Presentation Q&A (10 min)
7:05 - 7:15 | Closing Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min), call for speakers for next time, and raise your hand if you are hiring!
7:15 - 8:00 | Reception (45 min)

*🗣️Presentation Description: *

Description: In the dbt Labs on dbt series, you’ll get a behind-the-scenes look at how dbt Labs uses data. You’ll learn how dbt Labs thinks about the role of data, how data developers collaborate with business leaders, and the technical decisions we’ve made in our own dbt project.

In this session, Danny Lambert, Director of Marketing Operations at dbt Labs, will share how he's partnered with both marketing leaders and the data team to deliver clean, simple reporting that help everyone from campaign managers to the executive team make better decisions about marketing spend and optimization. Jakki Jakaj, Associate Manager of Integrated Marketing at dbt Labs, will share how her team uses this data every day to optimize marketing campaigns.

Speaker bio:
Meet Jakki Jakaj, a Marketing Guru at dbt Labs, based in Austin, TX. A true food enthusiast, Jakki combines her passion for marketing with her love for exploring culinary delights. In her role for over two years, she spearheads impactful Go-to-Market strategies that drive conversions and ensures every user enjoys a remarkable experience with dbt. When not immersed in work, Jakki treasures quality time with friends and family!

Danny Lambert, a seasoned marketer, has worked across large and small organizations in both the public and private sectors. Danny has been leading successful marketing and operations teams across various industries including healthcare, technology, e-commerce and more. He previously drove the strategy and execution of high-growth marketing initiatives as the Director of Integrated Marketing at Rev. Danny now leads marketing operations at dbt Labs building scalable systems, processes, and reporting that foster revenue growth.

dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

events.raw_text:

Mon, Oct 17, 2022, 1:00 PM to Fri, Oct 21, 2022, 1:00 AM CEST

Online event

This event has passed

Coalesce: The Analytics Engineering Conference
Network event

183 attendees from 16 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Austin dbt Meetup
4.8
16 reviews
Details

In case you missed it, dbt Labs is hosting Coalesce 2022 from October 17 - 21. It is the annual conference dedicated to advancing the practice of analytics engineering. Coalesce offers data practitioners an opportunity to deepen their knowledge and network. You can choose your own adventure including in-person, online, and hybrid experiences.

Coalesce Online (FREE) | October 17-21, 2022. The online experience that you know and love. Connect with data practitioners around the globe in sessions designed for North America, APAC, EMEA and LATAM regions. Catch live stream sessions from Coalesce New Orleans, interact with the community and speakers via Slack, and attend exclusively virtual sessions.
Coalesce New Orleans | October 17-21, 2022 | Located at the Hyatt Regency, New Orleans. Our first time together, in person! Attend a presentation, workshop, or panel discussion. Participate in activities, a hackathon, intimate discussions, and social activities. Meet community members and partners while exploring the history and culture of New Orleans.
Coalesce Online + | London, 20 October 2022 | Sydney, 21 October 2022. A one-day event taking place in London and Sydney, complementing Coalesce Online. This will include tech talks, hosted discussions, an Executive keynote and a post-event networking session.

⚠️Please note that Coalesce 2022 won't be hosted on Meetup.com.
✅REGISTER + SIGN UP for sessions here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com
📅Review the schedule here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com/schedule
⭐As a member of the dbt Meetup Network, please use your discount code MEETUP15 for Premium and GA tickets.

events.raw_text:

Thu, Jul 28, 2022 · 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM CDT

Easy Tiger

1501 E 7th St · Austin, TX

Austin dbt Meetup (in-person)

Hosted by Jakki J. and Laura E.

Austin dbt Meetup
4.8
16 reviews
Details

✨We are excited to help bring Texas-based folks and folks in the surrounding areas from the dbt Community together! Looking forward to socializing and seeing both new and familiar faces. ✨

Expect a happy hour, some bites, refreshments, and vibes.🍺🍷🥤 ☕🥗🍔🍕 No talks, no content.

🤝 Hosts:

Laura Ellis, Co-organizer of Data Mishaps Night & VP, Data Engineering & Platform Analytics @ Rapid7
Jakki Jakkaj, dbt Labs

🏠 Venue: Easy Tiger East @ 1501 E 7th St, Austin, TX 78702

👀 HOW TO FIND US: We have a section of tables near the back of the outdoor Park. There will be a sign labeled "dbt Community Meetup" right by our area to help you find our dedicated space.

✅Agenda
6:30pm | Folks arrive, drinks/food start
6:45/7:00pm | Intro remarks (Laura Ellis)
Until 7:30pm | Discussion groups (topics on tables)
7:30-8:30pm | Open mingling

There will be 5 conversation topics to guide you, one per table. We voted on the topics in #local-texas in dbt Slack. When you arrive, find your people! These are the topics:

Growth paths/career development for analytics professionals
Centralization vs. decentralization of data teams/analytics strategy
How to know when it’s time to learn a new tool or skill in the ever- expanding data ecosystem? Is there a metaskill of knowing when to learn something new?
What is the correct order of operations in acquiring tools/workflows to get a team on a modern analytics stack?
What are non-negotiables when it comes to your tools, team, role, the projects you take on?

***

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community:
https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝🏽 Be part of the conversation in the #local-texas channel in dbt Slack!
This is where the chat will also take place during meetups.

(COVID Information here) dbt Labs Meetup Policy: https://bit.ly/3z7LfTw

Online event

This event has passed

Austin dbt Meetup (Online)

Hosted by Rosie C.

Austin dbt Meetup
4.8
16 reviews
Details

✨We're excited to host a dbt Meetup in Texas!✨

Here's what to expect at this meetup:

Data Quality is A Team Sport!

We all care about data quality and no one will dispute its importance. But perhaps one of the greatest challenges in tackling this problem lies in the fact that data quality is a team sport. This meetup aims to start a conversation between teams that might not get a chance to talk to one another often (or at all) and suggests ideas on how to better understand stages in the data processes that impact or are impacted by our work.

✅Agenda

6:00 - Welcome Remarks & Announcements
6:10 - Coalesce Teaser w/ Icebreaker
6:15 - Proactive Data Observability by Harper
6:35 - Panel: Cross-team Collaboration in Data Quality w/ Carolyn + Harper
6:55 - Closing Remarks & Feedback Session
7:00 - Optional informal chat

➡️ Join our the dbt Slack community:
https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝🏽 Be part of the conversation in the #local-texas channel in dbt Slack!
This is where the chat will also take place during meetups.
